sourceMovie.title: Project X
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Project X (2012) as a comedy theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Back to the Future Part II
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Project X clicked for you, Back to the Future Part II (1989) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Project X?
faq.answer: Try Back to the Future Part II, Superbad, Grease—each shares DNA with Project X while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Project X?
faq.answer: Project X (2012) is best described as Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Project X worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
